title: Storybook ThemeProvider missing as global decorator
description: Storybook throws 'preview hooks can only be called inside decorators and story functions' error. Root cause: ThemeProvider added to component library (co-vz3) but not registered as a global decorator in .storybook/preview.ts. Fix: wrap all stories with ThemeProvider via a global decorator in preview.ts so useTheme and dark mode work correctly in every story.

title: vitest picks up test files from polecat worktrees causing React dual-instance failures
description: When running npm test from the components repo root, vitest discovers test files inside polecat git worktrees (e.g. polecats/slit/components/src/**/*.test.tsx). These worktrees have their own node_modules/react, causing 'Cannot read properties of null (reading useState)' errors due to React dual-instance collision. The vite.config.ts has no vitest 'include' pattern restricting test discovery to src/ only. Fix: add vitest config to restrict test files to src/ and exclude polecats/ directory.

title: Add dark mode toggle to Storybook
description: Install and configure storybook-dark-mode addon (or @storybook/addon-themes). All stories should render in both light and dark modes via toolbar toggle. Update existing stories (Button) and ensure new stories (DataTable, Spinner) include dark mode coverage.

title: Wire up ThemeProvider for dark mode support
description: Add shadcn ThemeProvider to the components library. Toggle dark class on root element. Ensure all existing components (Button, DataTable, Spinner) render correctly in both light and dark modes. This is a prerequisite for dark mode Storybook stories.

title: DataTable Phase 1
description: Build DataTable Phase 1 using TanStack Table v8. Requirements: typed column definitions, client-side sorting (single and multi-column), client-side filtering (global search + per-column text/select/date range/number range filters), client-side pagination with page size selector, column visibility toggle, row selection (single and multi with bulk actions toolbar), row-level actions (inline button or context menu), clickable rows (onRowClick), loading state (skeleton rows), empty state, error state, export to CSV, refresh button slot, custom toolbar action slot. Built with Tailwind + shadcn Table primitive. Include Storybook stories and Vitest tests.

title: Spinner/Loader component
description: Build a Spinner/Loader component using Tailwind CSS and shadcn primitives. Variants: sizes (sm, md, lg), colors (inherit from context or explicit). Should work as both a standalone spinner and an overlay loader for blocking UI. Include Storybook story and Vitest smoke test.

title: Storybook setup
description: Add Storybook to the component library. Install @storybook/react-vite, configure .storybook/main.ts and preview.ts, add a working Button story as a smoke test. Should run with 'npm run storybook'.
close_reason: Storybook setup complete and merged to main

title: Vitest + Testing Library setup
description: Add Vitest and @testing-library/react to the project. Configure vitest.config.ts (or extend vite.config.ts), add jsdom environment, add a smoke test for a basic component. Should run with 'npm run test'.

title: Component library scaffold
description: Initialize the React+TypeScript+Vite component library project. Set up package.json with React/TypeScript/Tailwind deps, tsconfig, vite.config.ts in library mode, and basic project structure (src/components/ui/, src/index.ts). Initialize shadcn/ui (components.json config, cn() utility, CSS variables). Should produce a buildable package before any components are added. Stack: React, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui.
close_reason: Scaffold complete and merged to main

title: styling: full-width search, Filters text+count button, X–Y of Z pagination
description:
attached_molecule: co-wisp-vtq6
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-19T13:28:33Z
attached_vars: ["setup_command=npm install","typecheck_command=npm run typecheck","lint_command=npm run lint","test_command=npm test","build_command=npm run build"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: setup_command=npm install
typecheck_command=npm run typecheck
lint_command=npm run lint
test_command=npm test
build_command=npm run build

Three visual polish changes to data-table.tsx:

1. **Full-width search**: Change search input from fixed `w-48` to `flex-1` so it expands to fill available toolbar space.

2. **Filters button → text + count**: Replace the icon-only SlidersHorizontal button with a text button that reads "Filters" when no filters are active, and "Filters 2" (or the active count) when column filters are applied. Keep the secondary variant when filtersOpen is true.
   - Show count badge when `columnFilters.length > 0`
   - Format: <SlidersHorizontal icon> "Filters" [count badge when > 0]

3. **Pagination label → X–Y of Z**: Replace "Page X of Y" with the row range + total format:
   - e.g., "1–25 of 100" for client-side mode
   - For server-side mode: same format using serverTotal
   - Formula: start = pageIndex * pageSize + 1, end = min(start + pageSize - 1, totalRows)

File to edit: `src/components/ui/data-table.tsx`

Also update `src/components/ui/data-table.test.tsx` to verify:
- Search input has class `flex-1` (not `w-48`)
- Filters button text reads "Filters" initially
- Filters button shows "Filters 2" when 2 column filters are active
- Pagination label shows "1–2 of 2" format

After edits: run `npm run build` to rebuild dist, commit all changes (src + dist).

title: styling: filter row collapse with active-filter chips
description: attached_molecule: co-wisp-h71w attached_formula: mol-polecat-work attached_at: 2026-04-19T13:02:03Z attached_vars: ["setup_command=npm install","typecheck_command=npm run typecheck","lint_command=npm run lint","test_command=npm test","build_command=npm run build"] dispatched_by: mayor formula_vars: setup_command=npm install typecheck_command=npm run typecheck lint_command=npm run lint test_command=npm test build_command=npm run build ## Why The column filter row is always visible even when no filters are active, consuming vertical space and making the toolbar feel cluttered. ## What 1. Hide the filter row by default (filtersOpen state = false). 2. Add a 'Filters' toggle button (SlidersHorizontal icon) to the toolbar that shows/hides the filter row. 3. When a column filter is active (non-empty value), show a dismissable chip in the toolbar instead of hiding the state from the user. ## Changes ### src/components/ui/data-table.tsx #### New state variable (add alongside existing state): const [filtersOpen, setFiltersOpen] = React.useState(false) #### New imports from lucide-react (add to existing import): import { SlidersHorizontal, X } from 'lucide-react' #### Toolbar — add Filters toggle button after the search input, before bulk actions: {enableColumnFilters && ( )} #### Active filter chips — add inside toolbar left div, after the toggle button: {enableColumnFilters && columnFilters.map((f) => { const colDef = columnDefs.find((c) => ((c as {accessorKey?:string}).accessorKey ?? c.id) === f.id) const label = typeof colDef?.header === 'string' ? colDef.header : f.id return ( {label} ) })} #### Column filter row — make conditional on filtersOpen: Current: the columnFilterRow variable is always rendered inside TableHeader New: only render columnFilterRow when filtersOpen is true i.e. change: {columnFilterRow} to: {filtersOpen && columnFilterRow} ## After changes 1. Run: npm run build (rebuilds dist/) 2. Run: vitest run — all tests must pass 3. Add tests for: - Filter row hidden by default (enableColumnFilters=true, no filter row inputs visible initially) - Clicking 'Toggle filters' button shows filter row - Setting a column filter creates a chip in the toolbar - Clicking chip X button clears the filter 4. Commit with message: 'styling: filter row collapse with active-filter chips (co-)' ## Tests - vitest run must pass including new filter-collapse tests

title: styling: icon-only toolbar buttons + inline search icon
description:
attached_molecule: co-wisp-5o8z attached_formula: mol-polecat-work attached_at: 2026-04-19T12:53:14Z attached_vars: ["setup_command=npm install","typecheck_command=npm run typecheck","lint_command=npm run lint","test_command=npm test","build_command=npm run build"] dispatched_by: mayor formula_vars: setup_command=npm install typecheck_command=npm run typecheck lint_command=npm run lint test_command=npm test build_command=npm run build ## Why The DataTable toolbar shows text buttons 'Export CSV' and 'Columns' which take up too much horizontal space. The search input has no visual affordance. Replace with compact icon-only buttons with tooltips. ## What 1. Add a Search icon inside the global filter input (left side). 2. Replace 'Export CSV' text button with Download icon + tooltip. 3. Replace 'Columns' text button with Columns2 icon + tooltip. 4. Add aria-label to each icon button so tests can find them by accessible name. 5. Update data-table.test.tsx assertions that currently use getByText('Columns') and getByText('Export CSV') — these will break when buttons become icon-only. ## Changes ### src/components/ui/data-table.tsx #### Imports — add to existing lucide-react import: import { Search, Download, Columns2 } from 'lucide-react' Note: lucide-react is already a peer dependency. #### Global filter input — wrap in a relative div and add the icon: Replace the current plain for the global filter with:
setGlobalFilter(e.target.value)} placeholder='Search…' className='h-9 w-48 rounded-md border border-input bg-background pl-8 pr-3 text-sm focus:outline-none focus:ring-1 focus:ring-ring' />
#### Export button — replace text with icon: Current: New: #### Columns button — replace text with icon: Current: New: ### src/components/ui/data-table.test.tsx #### Fix test: 'shows Columns button when enableColumnVisibility is true' Current: expect(screen.getByText('Columns')).toBeInTheDocument() New: expect(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /columns/i })).toBeInTheDocument() #### Fix test: 'toggles column visibility dropdown' Current: await user.click(screen.getByText('Columns')) New: await user.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /columns/i })) #### Fix test: 'shows Export CSV button when enableExport is true' Current: expect(screen.getByText('Export CSV')).toBeInTheDocument() New: expect(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /export csv/i })).toBeInTheDocument() ## After changes 1. Run: npm run build (rebuilds dist/) 2. Run: vitest run — all tests must pass 3. Commit with message: 'styling: icon-only toolbar buttons + inline search icon (co-)' ## Tests - vitest run must pass including the updated assertions

title: styling: table row hover accent + header uppercase/tracking
description:
attached_molecule: co-wisp-xn3d
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-19T12:49:13Z
attached_vars: ["setup_command=npm install","typecheck_command=npm run typecheck","lint_command=npm run lint","test_command=npm test","build_command=npm run build"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: setup_command=npm install
typecheck_command=npm run typecheck
lint_command=npm run lint
test_command=npm test
build_command=npm run build

## Why
Table rows have no hover feedback (the existing class is hover:bg-muted/50 which is very subtle) and column headers are in the same weight/size as body text, making the table hard to scan.

## What
1. Update TableRow hover class in table.tsx for a more visible but still subtle slate-50 hover.
2. Add uppercase + tracking-wide to TableHead in table.tsx so headers are clearly distinct from data.

## Changes

### src/components/ui/table.tsx

TableRow — change the existing hover:bg-muted/50 to hover:bg-slate-50 and add dark mode variant:
  Current: 'border-b transition-colors hover:bg-muted/50 data-[state=selected]:bg-muted'
  New:     'border-b transition-colors hover:bg-slate-50 dark:hover:bg-slate-800/50 data-[state=selected]:bg-muted'

TableHead — add uppercase text-xs tracking-wide to the existing className string:
  Current: 'h-10 px-3 text-left align-middle font-medium text-muted-foreground [&:has([role=checkbox])]:pr-0 [&>[role=checkbox]]:translate-y-[2px]'
  New:     'h-10 px-3 text-left align-middle font-medium text-muted-foreground uppercase text-xs tracking-wide [&:has([role=checkbox])]:pr-0 [&>[role=checkbox]]:translate-y-[2px]'

## After changes
1. Run: npm run build (rebuilds dist/)
2. Commit with message: 'styling: table row hover accent + header uppercase/tracking (co-<id>)'

## Tests
- vitest run must pass (no existing test checks these class strings)
- No data-table.test.tsx tests need updating for this bead
close_reason: Merged to main (d6242d0) — manually merged after gt done MR bead creation failure

title: vitest picks up test files from polecat worktrees causing React duplicate hooks failures
description: vitest.config.ts has no include/exclude patterns, so vitest globs all **/*.test.tsx including polecats/ subdirectories. Polecat worktrees have their own node_modules with separate React/react-dom copies, causing 'Cannot read properties of null (reading useState)' errors when those tests run. Pre-existing on main (not caused by any specific polecat branch). Fix: add exclude: ['polecats/**', 'witness/**', 'refinery/**', 'mayor/**'] to vitest config. Observed: 37 test failures from polecats/slit/components/src/components/ui/ (data-table.test.tsx x29, theme-provider.test.tsx x8). 123 tests pass in src/.

title: components rig patrol missing setup_command and test_command config
description: Patrol mol-refinery-patrol has setup_command=empty and test_command='go test ./...' defaults, but components is a Node.js/TypeScript project. Running tests requires npm install first (setup_command) and npm test (test_command). Without these, tests fail silently or with 'command not found'. The rig config.json should set: setup_command=npm install, test_command=npm test.

title: DataTable Phase 2
description: Extend DataTable with server-side mode: async fetch function API, server-side sorting (passes sort params to fetch), server-side filtering, server-side pagination. Also: row expansion (collapsible detail panel beneath row), pinned/frozen columns (left and right), built-in cell formatters (date, datetime, currency, number, boolean, badge/status), custom cell renderer escape hatch. Depends on DataTable Phase 1.

title: File Upload Phase 1
description:
attached_molecule: co-wisp-wblmn
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-21T17:22:57Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Build File Upload Phase 1 using react-dropzone for drag/drop mechanics. Requirements: click to browse + drag/drop zone, file type restrictions (accept prop), file size limits with error messaging, single and multi-file modes, per-file progress bar, success/error state per file, image preview thumbnails, non-image files show icon + filename + size, remove/clear file before upload, callback-based upload (consumer provides upload function — we don't own transport), react-hook-form compatible, controlled and uncontrolled modes, mobile fallback (tap to browse). Built with Tailwind + shadcn Progress and Button primitives. Include Storybook stories and Vitest tests.

title: Date Picker enhanced
description:
attached_molecule: co-wisp-gy2k
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work

Build an enhanced Date Picker on top of react-day-picker (already used by shadcn). Must fix known shadcn gaps: year/month jump navigation (no 360-click problem), datetime picker (date + time together), date range selection with presets, timezone handling, works correctly inside Dialog/Modal components. react-hook-form compatible. Built with Tailwind + shadcn Popover primitive. Include Storybook stories and Vitest tests.

title: Command Palette
description: Build a Command Palette (⌘K / Ctrl+K) component wrapping shadcn's Command primitive (cmdk). Requirements: keyboard shortcut trigger (Cmd/Ctrl+K), modal overlay, fuzzy search across registered commands, keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter to execute), command groups/sections, icons alongside command labels, programmatic API for registering commands from anywhere in the app. Built with Tailwind + shadcn Command + Dialog primitives. Include Storybook stories and Vitest tests.

title: File Upload Phase 2
description: Extend File Upload with: retry failed uploads, duplicate file detection with clear error messaging, improved non-image file previews (PDF, doc, etc. with appropriate icons). Depends on File Upload Phase 1.

title: Upgrade Storybook from v8 to v9+
description: Currently on storybook 8.6.14. v9 (May 2025) and v10 (Oct 2025) are both out. Main blocker is storybook-dark-mode addon incompatibility — needs replacing with @storybook/addon-themes and preview.ts dark mode logic rewrite. Everything else is automated via npx storybook@latest upgrade. Recommend v9 first, then v10.

title: DataTable Phase 3 — virtualization
description: Add row virtualization to DataTable using TanStack Virtual. For large client-side datasets where pagination is not desired. Renders only visible rows in viewport, removes offscreen rows from DOM. Backlog item — implement when a project specifically requires it.

value: Every component must have a Storybook story with explicit light AND dark mode story exports. Dark mode story is part of the definition of done for any new component — not optional.
